data_quality.detail: OnlyFans and Feabie have first snapshots only, so their current audience is excluded from organic growth and counted as newly tracked coverage.

data_quality.issue: Total change is not organic growth
data_quality.detail: 8,417 followers are from newly tracked platforms; organic change from accounts with history is +71.
Only 7/11 accounts have a full 7-day baseline. The longest observed history is 17 days.
Only 0/11 accounts have a full 30-day baseline. The longest observed history is 17 days.
Facebook Page (sample from Mar 6, 117 days old) use older owned-post samples. Current content recommendations are suppressed until newer media data is available.
Meta returned both a latest page-follows value (14,990) and a summed value across returned rows. The dashboard displays the latest value to avoid double-counting.

recommendations.label: Review what drove growth on Instagram
recommendations.text: Instagram gained +43 followers since the last update. Look at what was posted or promoted around that change and reuse the strongest pattern.

recommendations.label: Watch Facebook Page until the weekly trend is ready
recommendations.text: It changed -10 since the last update. One-day movement can be noisy, so treat it as a watch item.

recommendations.label: Review what worked on Instagram
recommendations.text: The latest usable sample has 147,691 engagements across 25 items. Reuse the strongest format before changing the offer.

recommendations.label: Keep your Instagram link steady
recommendations.text: 89 of 1,619 profile views clicked through (5.50%). Keep the link and bio offer stable this week so the next update is comparable.

recommendations.label: Treat new tracking coverage separately
recommendations.text: 8,417 followers came from newly connected tracking coverage. Use since-last-update change for day-to-day movement.

sources.note: Profile counts and owned-account media metrics require Instagram Graph API access for professional accounts.
Account insights are optional and require additional insights permissions.
Page follower, like, rating, and talking-about counts come from the Page node.
Page insights are optional, Page-token based, and may return empty data depending on metric availability.
follower_count is returned from /v2/user/info/ with user.info.stats authorization.
Subscriber counts come from channels.list statistics when public.
followers_count is available in public_metrics on user lookup responses.
Public store pages expose store-level Followers and Clips counts; this is public-page collection, not an official API feed.
Public profile pages can expose Followers when the creator's follower visibility is public; this is public-page collection, not an official API feed.
Public profile pages can expose Followers when visible; this is public-page collection, not an official API feed.
Follower count is currently tracked from user-visible profile totals as a manual snapshot until live collection is configured.
Fan count is currently tracked from user-visible subscriber totals as a manual snapshot until authenticated live collection is tested.
